strengths: Some success criteria are implied through the test plan
Basic acknowledgement of what would constitute product success
weaknesses: No scenario or client investigation presented
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The planning section lacks any realâworld scenario or client consultation (both at Band 0), but there is a limited set of success criteria embedded in the test plan (Band 1â2). Overall this equates to 2/6, reflecting minimal planning evidence.

strengths: Multiple interacting modules with authentication, AI integration, mood tracking and breathing exercises
Effective use of modern tools (MERN stack, JWT, OpenAI API)
weaknesses: Sparse source attribution and little explanation of implementation techniques
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Development shows a moderateâhigh level of complexity and very effective tool use (Band 5â8), but documentation of sources and techniques is weak (Band 1â4). Holistically this sits in the upper mid-range at 10/12.

strengths: Comprehensive design overview with ERD, data dictionary, UML, flowcharts and wireframes
Detailed and complete test plan covering functional and non-functional checks
weaknesses: No chronological record of tasks or development log provided
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: The solution overview is very strong in both design artefacts and testâplan detail (each at the top of the band), missing only the record of tasks. This justifies the full 6/6.

strengths: Modular API and data schema allow straightforward expansion
Claims of working features suggest a functional baseline
weaknesses: Functionality only partially demonstrated (no video verification)
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: Although full functionality could not be independently verified, the architecture and schema indicate a well-modularised product with straightforward extension. This aligns with the top of Band 3â4, meriting 4/4.

strengths: Some implicit evaluative thinking via test criteria
weaknesses: No explicit evaluation against success criteria
No client/adviser feedback or future recommendations
feedback.calculationMethod: Holistic AI evaluation: There is essentially no formal evaluation section (all strands sit at Band 0), but the existence of a detailed test plan implies some limited reflection on outcomes. This marginal insight warrants 2/6.

strandSelections.reasoning: The student supplies a rich set of artefacts: data dictionary, ERD, architecture diagram, UML class diagram, multiple flowcharts, API route table, and wireframes for every page. Together they give a clear, coherent picture of how the product will be built and how the components interact, matching the descriptors for a comprehensive overview.

No separate section explains why this product was chosen or how it best meets the clientâs needs. The design documents imply a mental-health chatbot, but the rationale is neither identified nor justified, so the strand sits in Band 0.

The design leverages a modern MERN-style stack, JWT, Bcrypt, local storage, OpenAI services and responsive web UI frameworks. Tool usage is appropriate, varied, and appears well integrated, fitting the âappropriate and effectiveâ description within Band 5-8; a high but not maximum mark is assigned because depth of configuration is not fully documented.

A structured test plan lists ten distinct functional and non-functional tests, each with actions and expected results. Coverage spans authentication, security, persistence, error handling and environment safety, satisfying every aspect of a detailed outline test plan.
No future improvements or maintenance recommendations are documented. The strand thus earns no marks.

Technical diagrams are well annotated, yet there is no bibliography, no citation of external tutorials, and scant explanation of how individual algorithms (e.g., breathing animation) were implemented. Some implicit sources (OpenAI, JWT) are mentioned but not properly referenced, placing the work in the middle of Band 1-4.
There is no chronological log or table of tasks completed over time. Without such a record, the strand earns no marks.

The proposed system integrates user authentication, JWT security, MongoDB data storage, AI-powered chat using OpenAI, mood-tracking with calendar visualisation, and an animated breathing exercise. This shows multiple interacting modules and external APIs, evidencing a moderate but not exceptional degree of complexity and ingenuity (mid-Band 5-8).

Numbered tests in the plan (criteria 1-10) imply that some success measures exist, yet these criteria are not presented as an upfront, clearly worded list. Several are too technical (â.env not committedâ) to be genuine client-focused success metrics. Hence only a limited and partly inappropriate set is evident, meriting the bottom of Band 1-2.
There is no transcript, summary, or screenshot of feedback from a client or adviser. Consequently, Band 0 applies.

The modular API design (separate endpoints for chat, mood, breathing) and clear data schema indicate that new featuresâadditional mental-health tools, analytics, etc.âcould be added without major restructuring. The degree of modularity meets the descriptor for straightforward expansion, earning Band 3-4 but not the maximum because no explicit extension examples are provided.

The submission excerpt contains no description of a real-world scenario, no explicit identification of a client, and no evidence that the student consulted anyone. The narrative begins directly with technical artefacts (data dictionary, diagrams, test plan). Consequently, the strand meets none of the minimum requirements for Band 1-2.

Without the demonstration video, full functionality cannot be confirmed. The documentation claims that each feature works, suggesting partial evidence, so the strand aligns with âproduct functions partially as demonstratedâ (Band 1-2). A midpoint score reflects that several independent modules are listed.
No section evaluates the finished product against success criteria. The strand therefore receives zero.

annotations.comment: Include annotations for interactive elements (e.g., tap/click areas) on the wireframe to specify user interactions.
The calendar grid description is vague. Provide grid cell dimensions and label conventions to improve clarity of layout.
The chat page table outlines layout components effectively but omits responsive behavior details, limiting UI planning scope.
Add notes on responsive design breakpoints and layout adjustments to ensure the UI adapts for mobile and desktop.
The home page mockup shows mood tracking UI clearly, aiding understanding of user flow on first load.
The duplicate âChat Pageâ header reduces clarity; use consistent section naming to avoid reader confusion.
The submission lacks any description of a real-world scenario, identified client, or evidence of consultation. Consider adding a clear scenario and client investigation to contextualize the project.
The data dictionary comprehensively lists collections, fields, types, constraints, and descriptions, demonstrating a detailed design artefact.
Relationships between collections (e.g., users to conversations) are implied by references but not explicitly described. Add narrative on foreign-key relationships to clarify data integrity.
Consider explaining indexing strategies or performance considerations for large-scale data retrieval to enhance the data dictionaryâs practical utility.
The nested messageSchema and mood collections show attention to modelling complex structures, enhancing the overall design detail.
The ERD clearly depicts core entities and their connections, supporting a coherent overview of the database model.
Include cardinality notation (e.g., 1-to-many) on ERD connectors to specify relationship multiplicities explicitly.
The architecture diagram effectively shows data flow between frontend, backend, database, and OpenAI API, illustrating system components.
The UML class diagram isolates classes and attributes clearly, facilitating understanding of object structure.
The repeated âUML Class Diagramâ header appears twice without differentiation, which may confuse readers. Remove duplicates or rename sections.
Annotate the chat flow diagram with clear start and end symbols and decision labels to improve readability.
The breathing exercise flowchart outlines each phase clearly, supporting implementation of timed inhale, hold, and exhale states.
Label decision points with the specific timer durations or conditions (e.g., inhale: 4s) to increase precision.
The mood update flowchart maps user actions to system responses in a clear sequence, aiding developer understanding.
The API routes table for auth endpoints is clear, but other endpoints are listed elsewhere. Consolidate all routes to avoid fragmentation.
Consider merging all endpoint definitions into a single table or section to present a unified interface specification.
The chat and mood endpoint table lists methods and auth requirements succinctly, aiding readability of API design.
The login/register wireframe lacks explicit field labels, making it hard to interpret where input elements should appear.
Annotate the wireframe with text labels on form fields and buttons to clarify expected UI elements.
Home page wireframe depicts main navigation and mood summary clearly, supporting user-centred design planning.
Unify labeling conventions and font styling across wireframes to present a consistent design language.
The UI mockups for the mood calendar and breathing pages provide realistic visual previews, aiding implementation alignment.
Consider annotating final UI images with expected asset dimensions, DPI, and color codes to guide developers.
The test plan enumerates a wide range of checks with clear actions and expected results, demonstrating thorough test coverage.
The test criteria reference numeric IDs but lack a preceding list of client-focused success criteria. Define those criteria explicitly.
Link each test back to a clear success criterion statement to allow evaluation of outcomes against client needs.
